REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (6 Longview Court, San Francisco, Ca 94131)
Off Calendar Dec-09-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Lawrence Siracusa, for and with petitioner Debra Dolch.

Mr. Siracusa stated that the proposed sale was for a 100% interest in the real property located at 8 Longview Court, San Francisco, California, for the bid amount of $955,000.00, upon the terms and conditions of the report of sale filed with the court on November 5, 2015. He stated that increased bond of $908,000.00 was needed at that sale price. There were no overbidders.
The sale was confirmed at $955,000.00 based on the terms and conditions as set forth in the report of sale. The Court granted the Petition to Confirm Sale of Real Property (subject to review of the order). An additional bond of $908,000.00 was required.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not reported)

Petition For Authority To List And Sell Trust'S Real Property
Off Calendar Aug-17-2015 Granted Per Written Order

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #2, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Mar-25-2015 Granted Per Written Order

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #2, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Mar-09-2015 Continued To Mar-23-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Andrew Schultz for (trustee) Debra Dolch.

Mr. Schultz gave a brief history of the way the conservatorship operated in regards to Ms. Ford having control of her assets, that the plan was to let her remain financially independent, that the reason the credit card balances were high was due to extensive travel and that Ms. Dolch reviewed all statements. Ms. Dolch reported that Ms. Ford became ill 2 years ago while in Alaska, that construction was being done to the house while she was away, that there was a high cleaning cost at the time Ms. Ford returned as the cleaning person was coming in twice per week because of the construction dust, that credit card expenses are down significantly as Ms. Ford is no longer traveling and that the items purchased on the Macy's credit card are returned after purchase. The court requested that a declaration be filed with this information. The accounting petition was continued to March 23, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The matter will be deemed submitted at that time if the declaration is sufficient.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Allen. (Not Reported)
